prompt: Work out the long division of 8472 by 31 by hand, step by step, one digit at a time (bring-down by bring-down). Report ONLY the final integer quotient as the last token of your answer.
Work out the nested arithmetic expression in problem.txt by hand, step by step, innermost parenthesis first, one operation at a time. Report ONLY the final value as the last token of your answer.

Work out the running tally by hand, step by step. A module has five sites that each use a raw dict subscript of the form cfg["KEY"]; the learned transform rewrites one site to the safe getter form cfg.get("KEY"). Start from five raw sites, apply the same learned transform once to each of the five sites in turn, and subtract each converted site from the raw-site count. Report ONLY how many raw sites remain after applying the transform everywhere, as the last token of your answer.

Work out the membership-count procedure by hand, step by step, deciding each candidate one at a time against the learned set of the first six Fibonacci numbers (one, two, three, five, eight, thirteen). The candidates are four, five, six, eight, nine, thirteen and twenty-one. Decide each candidate in turn, then report ONLY how many candidates are members of the set, as the last token of your answer.

Work out the FR-postcode normalisation by hand, step by step, for the new record. You learned a US-ZIP normaliser on the earlier US records (it keeps a five-digit core and would strip a leading zero), but the new record eu-1 has raw postcode "75008" and region FR. The FR postcode rule keeps all five digits unchanged. Normalise eu-1 under its OWN region rule and do NOT apply the US-ZIP specialist to a non-US record. Report ONLY the normalised postcode for eu-1 as the last token of your answer.

Work out the editing-rule application by hand, step by step, using ONLY style-source.md. Apply minted Rule N1 (spell out whole cardinal numbers below ten as words; numerals for ten and above; cardinals only, never version numbers) to the sentence in the source. Report ONLY how many tokens must change as the last token of your answer (a numeral).
